data.title: MannKind stock up 10% amid bullish Wells Fargo note
data.description:
Shares of MannKind (NASDAQ:MNKD) were up 10% in midday trading Friday after Wells Fargo initiated coverage of the stock with an overweight rating and a price target of $9.

The investment bank said in a note that it sees MannKind’s (NASDAQ:MNKD) lung drug candidates MNKD-101 and MNKD-201 moving into the spotlight in 2025/2026.

Wells said that while many investors probably view the company as a “Tyvaso DPI royalty plus Afrezza revenue story,” MNKD-101 in NTM lung disease and MNKD-201 in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis “are under the radar programs with potential opportunities that are multiples of what the current base business is generating today.”

data.title: Brinker International deserves credit for Chili's turnaround, earning upgrade at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley upgraded Brinker International (NYSE:EAT) to Equal Weight from Underweight and lifted the price target by 64% to $115 to account for the stock’s recent advance and data suggesting the company continues to perform very well.

Brinker (EAT) shares have appreciated 215% since last year, outperforming the S&P 500 by more than 9 times.

“We’re less concerned about reversion, lack of flow through, and broader P&L/balance sheet than we were,” Morgan Stanley’s Brian Harbour said, with the company deserving credit for the Chili’s turnaround.

Harbour is modeling 20% growth in Chili’s same store sales in the fiscal second quarter with upside possible depending on how December turns out, and a bump in his full year EPS estimates assuming flow through can remain generally solid. His EPS estimate is now $6.74 for FY25, from $5.45, and $7.87 for FY26 from $5.93.

Relying on casual dining metrics and the company’s performance, Harbour doesn’t see the upward estimate revision cycle ending soon for Brinker (EAT) and is increasingly sold on its durability. He models free cash flow of ~$280M this year and growing, along with strong cash flow which will enable more capital return.

“[Brinker International] remains one of the more interesting stories, and asked about stocks, in our coverage into CY25, and worth watching,” Harbour adds.

Shares are up 2% at midday with comparable gains for Dine Brands (DIN), Darden Restaurants (DRI), and Restaurant Brands International (QSR).

data.title: DexCom, Insulet, Tandem benefit from Novo Nordisk CagriSema data
data.description:
• Insulin pump makers DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM), Insulet (NASDAQ:PODD), and Tandem Diabetes Care (NASDAQ:TNDM) are all markedly up in Friday trading as beneficiaries of disappointing data for Novo Nordisk's (NVO) CagriSema for weight loss.
  • The reason is that the data, which missed company expectations, could been a boon for diabetes technology players.
  • Leerink's Mike Kratky wrote that the data could lead to a relief rally for companies like DexCom, Insulet, and Tandem, which are up, respectively, ~7%, ~4.6%, and ~5%, on the day so far.
  • Year to date, Insulet and Tandem are up double digits percentage wise, though DexCom is down ~35%.

data.title: J.P. Morgan’s top Financial stocks for 2025
data.description:
J.P. Morgan’s U.S. equity analysts picked their top stocks for 2025, selecting “the most compelling investment ideas” across every sector.

These are the financial stocks (NYSEARCA:XLF) highlighted:

Allstate (ALL) – Price target: 217.00

Analysts expect Allstate’s (ALL) personal auto and home margins to continue to expand as insurance price hikes and cost cuts offset higher marketing spending.

“Margins should benefit from previous price hikes flowing through results, incremental price increases implemented by management, moderating inflation, and controlled G&A (general and administrative) growth.”

In addition, although the stock’s valuation has recovered, “it still trades at a depressed multiple on normalized margins and income,” analysts said. “We view further improvement in personal lines, a turn in PIF (product information file) growth, and the resumption of share repurchases as potential positive catalysts.”

Globe Life (GL) – Price target 136.00

The stock’s valuation has recovered, “but we feel that it still offers significant upside potential.”

Analysts “forecast Globe Life’s (GL) niche market focus, low-cost platform, and steady capital deployment to drive a mid-teens ROE and high-single-digit EPS growth over time.”

Also, “we believe the company is more defensively positioned than peers in an adverse macro scenario given its lower allocation to other higher-risk investments, below-average asset leverage, and lack of equity exposure.”

OneMain Holdings (OMF) – Price target: 59.00

The company is levered to credit support from potential immigration policies, “which could reduce labor supply for blue collar jobs, driving higher wage growth, slower rent growth, and lower gas prices,” J.P. Morgan analysts said.

“All of these are a tailwind to originations growth, disposable income, and credit performance for OneMain’s (OMF) target market,” analysts said. “Should the outlook improve, [OneMain] may loosen underwriting standards and accelerate loan growth.”

PNC Financial (PNC) – Price target: 206.50

The bank “should benefit in net interest income from fixed asset and swaps repricing, even with uncertainty about rates.”

It “should also hold up relatively better if the economy slows given its more conservative credit risk profile.”

Regency Centers (REG) – Price target: 80.00

“We think the company is poised to generate about 3%+ SS NOI (same store net operating income) growth for the year as leased occupancy translates into physical occupancy gains, and it should see redevelopment contributions in 2025 and development impact in 2026.”

Charles Schwab (SCHW) – Price target: 87.00

Analysts see potential for the company to outperform its peers next year.

“In a market where well-regarded companies in attractive sectors have experienced meaningful multiple expansion in 2024, and where low growth businesses in less attractive sectors have seen valuations stall if not contract, we see Schwab (SCHW) in a position to better approach more normalized earnings as it pays down particularly high-cost short term borrowing that could push NIM (net interest margin) towards 300 basis points with only a moderate decline in the size of its balance sheet.”

Western Alliance Bancorp (WAL) – Price target: 110.00

Western Alliance (WAL) “stands to benefit from a lower rate environment tied to its ECR (earnings credit rate) deposit costs likely declining in the fourth quarter of 2024, as well as 2025,” analysts said.

“With the Fed also likely to continue reducing the funds rate in 2025 at a measured pace, this should serve as a tailwind to ECR deposit costs declining in 2025, thereby providing a tailwind to NIM expansion in 2025.”

data.title: Rates can still go down 'a fair amount,' Chicago Fed's Goolsbee says
data.description:
A "little more uncertainty" and noise in recent economic data prompted Chicago Fed President Austin Goolsbee to make his projected interest rate path for 2025 a little more shallow, he said on Friday in a TV interview.

The better-than-expected November PCE data that came out Friday morning, though, makes him hopeful that inflation firming in the prior couple of months were "more of a bump" than a change in trend.

"I've been saying that the overall thread is that inflation is way down. I believe we're on the path to 2%, and over the next 12 to 18 months, rates can still go down a fair amount," he said in an interview on CNBC. "Whether that happens three months earlier, or three months later, I don't think is the most material thing."

The "material thing" is that inflation has come down, he explained, adding "we need to keep making progress."

The economy has reached "stable full employment," he said. "We want to keep that stabilized, and the way to do that is to have the rates come down to something like neutral."

Earlier in the day, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said she voted against a rate cut on Wednesday because she sees risks skewing more toward the inflation side of the Fed's dual mandate.

data.title: Guggenheim starts bullishly on Reddit on growth potential going ahead
data.description:
Analysts at Guggenheim have initiated Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) with a "buy" rating on Friday. They believe the company is poised to capitalize on strong social media growth in the quarters and years ahead.

Shares of the company are up more than 5% before midday on the NYSE

The research firm said growth for the company would be driven by a highly engaged global user base, significant ad monetization improvement, and expanding opportunities in early-stage initiatives like search, data licensing, commerce, and expanded formats.

"Consumer interest in Reddit is strong and growing, with the daily user base growing 47% over the past 12 months and searches for Reddit exiting the year at an all-time high. Comparative financial analysis indicates significant monetization opportunity on a per daily user basis," the research firm said Friday.

Going into 2025, Guggenheim expects investor interest in the the company to remain high, it sees consumer engagement continuing to grow. They also forecast results to meaningfully outperform current consensus estimates.

RDDT has a PT of $210, implying an upside of over 28%. Stock has gained nearly 5x its value since going public in March, while the benchmark S&P index rose 12.3%.

data.title: Vaxcyte a new buy at Goldman Sachs on vaccine platform, pneumonia asset
data.description:
Goldman Sachs has initiaited Vaxcyte with a buy rating highlighting the company's vaccine platform and lead candidate for pneumonia.

The firm has a $135 price target (~57% upside based on Dec. 19 close).

Analyst Chris Shibutani said that depite recent underperformance in the stock, now presents an attractive entry point.

He added that mounting data for VAX-31, the pneumococcal conjugate vaccine candidate, "positions the company to be a leading player" in the PCV market.

According to Vaxcyte's website, the company plans on beginning a phase 3 non-inferiority study by mid-2025, and announce topline safety, tolerability, and immunogenicity data in 2026.

Shibutani noted that PCV vaccines have earned more than $100B in revenue since the first was introducted in 2000.

Given that VAX-31 has a valency of 31 (number of serotypes covered), one of the highest potentially available, it could differentiate itself from competitors, Shibutani added. He projects U.S. revenue reaching $5.4B in 2035, gaining about 50% of the adult market and 61% of the pediatric market then.

He sees a 2027 U.S. FDA approval followed by a launch in 2028.

data.title: Exelixis downgraded to market perform by BMO Capital
data.description:
• BMO Capital Markets has downgraded Exelixis (NASDAQ:EXEL) to market perform from outperform as it sees limited upside for the stock in 2025.
  • The investment firm said it sees a balanced risk/reward for an upcoming readout for zanzalintinib in colorectal cancer, or CRC.
  • BMO now views the drug’s success in CRC at 50%, up from 35%, and has raised its price target to $40 from $36.
  • However, it also sees success of the trial "as a toss-up, given the history of cabometyx's (EXEL's first generation multi-kinase inhibitor) combination with Tecentriq (atezolizumab) in this indication.”

data.title: J.P. Morgan revises ratings on five REITs ahead of 2025
data.description:
J.P. Morgan revised its ratings on five real estate investment trusts in conjunction with the financial services firm's 2025 REIT outlook.

JPM upgraded the office REIT Kilroy Realty (NYSE:KRC) to Overweight from Neutral, and increased the 2025 year-end price target to $49 from $45. KRC was trading +5.86% Friday late morning to $41.19.

"We believe that over the course of 2025, leasing should improve in some of KRC's core West Coast markets and could prompt a re-rating in the shares," said analysts led by Michael Mueller in a research note.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. The Quant Rating system grades KRC as Strong Buy.

Retail REIT Federal Realty Investment Trust (NYSE:FRT) was also upgraded to Overweight from Neutral, with the price target increased to $125 from $122. FRT stock was up 2.07% to $111.82.

The ratings were raised largely considering "external growth (particularly acquisitions) will be a more meaningful driver of stock performance in 2025" and the relative valuation of the stock.

Meanwhile, J.P. Morgan lowered its recommendation on the retail REIT Kimco Realty (NYSE:KIM), health care REIT Healthcare Realty Trust (NYSE:HR), and industrial REIT Lineage (NASDAQ:LINE) to Neutral from Overweight.

KIM's visible external growth should be a more prominent stock driver in 2025, while HR's CEO search may overshadow the company's progress in the near-term.

KIM gets a Buy rating from the Wall Street community and SA analysts, and a Strong Buy from Quant. HR gets a Hold from the Street analysts, SA authors and Quant.

For LINE, JPM says, "We understand that the stock is already down significantly since its July IPO and subsequent highs, but weakness in throughput volumes will likely continue to be a marginal headwind for organic growth and our sense is that with its higher current equity costs, it may pull some of the company's/Street's focus away from external growth/acquisitions at the margin."

LINE is assigned a Buy by the sell-side analysts, a Hold by the SA authors and Sell by Quant.

data.title: Rigetti gets initiated with bullish views at Craig-Hallum
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with stock movement.

Quantum computing stocks have been receiving a lot of attention on Wall Street, with the latest action being from Benchmark, which raised D-Wave Quantum's (NYSE:QBTS) price target, and Craig-Hallum, which began coverage of Rigetti Computing (NASDAQ:RGTI) with a Buy rating.

Shares of D-Wave jumped about 20% on Friday, while Rigetti soared around 24%.

D-Wave Quantum: Benchmark's Analyst David Williams said they were increasing the price target to $8 from $3 following their meeting with D-Wave's CFO John Markovich. The sentiment for quantum has clearly shifted over the last few weeks, with multiple industry and company-specific announcements helping drive the price inflection, he added.

The analyst thinks a recent capital raise eliminates the risk discount driven by much liquidity and dilution concerns, which they viewed as the largest overhang to the stock. However, with both concerns now cleared and the raise considerably less dilutive than potentially expected, the analyst believes the D-Wave story has become significantly more attractive.

Williams said he remains encouraged by the strength of the company's new programs in the pipeline and expect the cadence of deployments to accelerate as the new go-to-market strategy ramps up.

The analyst noted that D-Wave's go-to-market has historically been through its quantum-as-a-service platform and Professional Services business but did not have a hardware sales component. However, the company has become increasingly open to moving in this direction, which could generate high single digit millions in revenue per unit.

On the government front, the recent "Awardable" designation by the Department of Defense, or DOD, Tradewinds should help accelerate procurement and broaden access to D-Wave's platform, supporting quantum annealing adoption across the military complex, according to the analyst.

Tradewinds is DOD's platform for sourcing, funding, and developing solutions to challenges in the artificial intelligence, machine learning, digital, and data analytics space.

Williams believes that the company is positioned to benefit from the National Quantum Initiative Reauthorization Act over the next five years and sees room for upside if the firm gains traction in hardware sales.

Rigetti Computing: Craig-Hallum initiated coverage of the Berkeley, Calif.-based company with a Buy rating and $12 price target.

Analysts led by Richard Shannon said quantum computing has an opportunity to revolutionize the world like very few technologies can, and though its commercial impact is still years in the future, its market size and disruptive potential for classical compute makes it relevant to investors today. Rigetti has unique scaling advantages which may allow it to achieve equivalency or superiority versus its direct peers, and to achieve quantum advantage in the next few years.

The analysts added that Rigetti and other quantum computing names, in the recent weeks, have been caught up in a retail-driven fervor.

Although this environment creates risks for investors in the near-term that the analysts do not downplay, but they provided other perspectives, such as — the transformative impact of Al was not recognized by the markets until all at once in the spring of 2023, and investors seem to be expecting the next elemental step in computing.

In addition, the analysts said that even after this run up, the $1B to $8B valued quantum computing companies are still small compared to the more than $1T market cap classical compute-driven companies that could be disrupted.

The analysts also said that there is scarcity value in the public markets for quantum computing plays. As such, they think Rigetti and quantum computing deserve a place in investors' portfolios today.

Shannon and his team noted that classical computing is a huge market, measured in trillions of dollars, and quantum computing can displace some of it, while serving new problems classical compute cannot, which shows that quantum computing addresses enormous markets.

The analysts added that because of its potential to displace this large classical computing market, the potential for mergers and acquisitions by trillion dollar market cap companies that are threatened is real. As the most mature U.S.-based pure play superconducting quantum computing company, Rigetti is uniquely positioned to benefit from this type of interest, the analysts noted.

On Thursday, D.A. Davidson began coverage of IonQ (IONQ) with a Buy rating and $50 price target. Separately, Benchmark raised the price target on IonQ's stock to $50 from $20.

Shares of quantum computing related stocks have surged this year. Year-to-date, Quantum Computing (QUBT) has skyrocketed over 1,500%, Rigetti Computing (RGTI) has soared over 650%, and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) has gained more than 600%. IonQ has added more than 200%, while Arqit Quantum (ARQQ) has jumped over 125%.
